mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 diesel engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Mercedes-Benz G250/G300 diesel engine costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only, varying by generation: OM602 £2,500–£4,500, OM603 £3,500–£6,000, OM606 £4,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 diesel reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM602, OM603 and OM606 are among Mercedes' most legendary diesel engines. The OM602 five-cylinder is simple, robust and widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most reliable engines. The OM603 six-cylinder is smooth but has head cracking issues. The OM606 turbo has exceptional reliability and good performance. The 300 TD OM606 is considered the best diesel G-Class.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 diesel?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM603: cylinder head cracking (coolant loss, overheating, £2,000–£4,500). All: timing chain wear (cold-start rattle, £800–£2,000), vacuum pump failure (hard brake pedal, £300–£800), glow plug failure (hard starting, £200–£500), and injection pump wear (£800–£1,500).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 diesel engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: G-Class values range from £15,000–£60,000+ depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £3,500–£6,500 reconditioned engine represents 10–40% of vehicle value — well within our 70% Mercedes-Benz scrap threshold. For almost any example, replacement is financially viable. The G-Class is a vehicle that holds its value exceptionally well.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 diesel engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G250/G300 diesel has used four engine variants: OM602 (W460/W461, 1979–2001, 2.5L 5-cyl, 94–125bhp), OM603 (W463, 1990–1994, 3.0L 6-cyl, 113bhp), and OM606 (W463, 1996–2000, 3.0L 6-cyl turbo, 177bhp). Check your VIN or registration year. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM603 cylinder head cracking issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: A cracked OM603 head can sometimes be repaired by a specialist (welding or stitching) costing £2,000–£4,500 — but in many cases, a replacement head or complete engine is more cost-effective (£3,500–£6,000). The #14 head casting is particularly susceptible. At the first sign of coolant loss or overheating, stop driving immediately and book an inspection. Prevention through cooling system maintenance is critical.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 Diesel Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges across all G-Class diesel generations. The OM606 turbo commands a premium over naturally aspirated OM602/OM603 units. Reconditioned G-Class engines are specialist items. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a G-Class diesel engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Mercedes specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). The G-Class's ladder-frame chassis provides reasonable engine bay access.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 Diesel — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only viable on low-mileage examples; often cheaper to replace the engine
Critical safety repair — always worth doing

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 Diesel? Check These First
buyIf: * OM606 300 TD (1996–2000) — the most powerful and reliable G-Class diesel
* Documented timing chain history — chains replaced or inspected
* Full service history with regular oil changes (5,000–7,500 miles)
* Cooling system proactively maintained (especially important on OM603)
avoidIf: * OM603 with any evidence of coolant loss or overheating — head cracking risk is serious
* Any cold-start rattle without documented timing chain work
* Poor service history — these engines are robust but not invincible
* Vacuum pump not recently replaced — failure is common and can be dangerous

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The G250/G300 diesel is a legend — the original Mercedes G-Class with some of the most durable diesel engines ever built. The OM602 five-cylinder is simple, robust and long-lived. The OM603 six-cylinder is smooth but has head cracking issues. The OM606 turbo is the pick of the bunch — 177hp, exceptional reliability and the most sought-after G-Class diesel. Buy the OM606 if you can, or a well-maintained OM602 with documented history. The G-Class is a vehicle that rewards patience and maintenance.

cta.label: → Compare Mercedes-Benz G250/G300 diesel engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz G250 / G300 Diesel (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain wear warning)
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — see the Era Map above
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — OM603 head cracking risk is serious
* Overheating History — Any evidence of overheating? (OM603 head cracking risk)
* Vacuum Pump — Any hard brake pedal or oil leaks? (vacuum pump failure)
* Glow Plugs — Any hard starting in cold weather? (glow plug failure)
* Injection Pump — Any hard starting or black smoke? (pump wear)
* Rust Check — G-Class chassis and body are prone to rust; body condition is critical
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
